channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: White House Releases Results of Trump’s Latest Physical Exam
item.description: The report by President Trump’s physician said he was in “excellent health.” It showed that he had gained weight and that neurological and heart tests had come back “normal.”
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: Reed, Walter, National Military Medical Center
item.media:credit: Jose Luis Magana
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week. The results of his recent physical exam were released late Friday.

item.title: Homelessness Declined in 2024, According to Delayed Federal Report
item.description: The modest drop marked the first decrease in homelessness in nearly a decade. The Housing and Urban Development report was published months later than usual.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle
item.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
item.media:description: A homeless encampment in Los Angeles. Though homelessness declined in 2024, it is still at unusually high levels by historical standards.

item.title: ICE Protesters Convicted in a Spokane Case That Tested a Trump Strategy
item.description: The three demonstrators in Spokane, Wash., had been accused of conspiracy, charges that the region’s former top prosecutor has said were unwarranted.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:credit: Rajah Bose for The New York Times
item.media:description: Downtown Spokane, Wash., in 2022. The city’s mayor called the verdict in the protesters’ case “politically motivated.”

item.title: Actually, Most Immigrants Won’t Need to Leave U.S. to Get Green Cards, D.H.S. Says
item.description: But details remained scant after officials said last week that, with “extraordinary” exceptions, people seeking permanent residency must first leave the country.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz, Madeleine Ngo and Lydia DePillis
item.media:credit: Valerie Plesch for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Department of Homeland Security appeared to make a partial walk-back of a change announced last Friday.

item.title: Trump Endorses Pamela Evette in South Carolina Governor’s Race
item.description: Pamela Evette had been among the most vocal proponents of redrawing the state’s congressional map to help Republicans in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs and Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Meg Kinnard/Associated Press
item.media:description: President Trump on Friday endorsed South Carolina’s lieutenant governor, Pamela Evette, to be the state’s next governor.

item.title: What We Know (and Don’t) About the E. Jean Carroll Lawsuits Inquiry
item.description: Prosecutors would face substantial hurdles in potentially pursuing charges against Ms. Carroll, who twice won cases against Donald Trump, or the billionaire who helped pay her lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak and Benjamin Weiser
item.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
item.media:description: E. Jean Carroll arrived for closing arguments in her defamation trial against Donald Trump in Manhattan in 2023.

item.title: Judge Reopens Trump’s Lawsuit Demanding $10 Billion From IRS
item.description: The ruling was a blow to both President Trump, who had voluntarily dismissed the suit last week, and to the Justice Department, which used the suit to establish a fund likely intended for Trump allies.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer and Andrew Duehren
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: In a stern order on Friday, a judge said that she wanted to investigate the circumstances surrounding President Trump’s efforts to settle the lawsuit in a way that benefited him and his allies.

item.title: Can Talarico win over some of Cornyn’s voters in Texas?
item.description: Democrats hope so, but there are good reasons to be skeptical.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:credit: Danielle Villasana/Getty Images
item.media:description: James Talarico

item.title: Bondi, Pressed Over Epstein Files, Places Responsibility on Blanche and Patel
item.description: Her remarks, delivered during a closed-door interview before the House Oversight Committee, were a candid admission of her own powerlessness.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush and Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi arrives for the House Oversight Committee closed hearing on Friday.

item.title: Surveillance Ordered for Hantavirus Ship Passengers Who Go Home
item.description: Trump administration officials want local health authorities to constantly monitor the 18 passengers for another three weeks, a requirement that far exceeds typical protocols.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin and Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Dylan Widger/Getty Images
item.media:description: The passengers who have been housed in the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center were aboard a cruise ship that became the center of a global hantavirus outbreak this month.

item.title: Journalist Favored by Trump Is in Talks to Join White House in Temporary Role
item.description: John Solomon said he was being vetted for a role as a “special government employee,” a status given to part-time, outside advisers to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Luke Broadwater
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates
Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
item.media:credit: Celal Gunes/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The journalist John Solomon spoke at the Conservative Political Action Conference in Maryland in 2023.

item.title: Judge Tosses Citizenship Law Aimed at New Voters in New Hampshire
item.description: The judge said the 2024 law “constitutes an unjustifiable burden on the right to vote,” and that new voters should be able to prove their citizenship with a sworn affidavit.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: People cast their ballots in Lancaster, N.H., in 2024.

item.title: Who Is the Prosecutor Investigating E. Jean Carroll’s Lawsuits?
item.description: Andrew S. Boutros, the Trump-appointed U.S. attorney for the Northern District of Illinois, is said to be overseeing the criminal investigation.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman and Danny Hakim
item.media:credit: E. Jason Wambsgans/Chicago Tribune, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Andrew S. Boutros, the U.S. attorney for the Northern District of Illinois, in Chicago in April.

item.title: Gretchen Whitmer’s Approach to Trump Was Working With the President
item.description: Ms. Whitmer, the Michigan governor and one of the country’s most prominent Democrats, has been conciliatory with Mr. Trump. As she mulls the future, her party is split on that approach.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer of Michigan stood beside President Donald Trump at Selfridge Air National Guard Base in Michigan last year.

item.title: 3 Latvian Climbers Die in Fall on Mt. McKinley
item.description: The accident occurred at about 18,000 feet in the area of Denali Pass.
item.dc:creator: John Branch
item.media:credit: Jim Wilson/The New York Times
item.media:description: The summit of Mount McKinley, in Alaska.

item.title: Ex-Iowa School Leader Who Was in the U.S. Illegally Sentenced to 2 Years
item.description: Ian Roberts, the former Des Moines superintendent, climbed high in the American education system without legal status in the country.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Kathryn Gamble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ian Roberts, former superintendent of Des Moines Public Schools, had jumped from job to job, lying about his citizenship.

item.title: Louisiana Approves Map Eliminating a Majority-Black District
item.description: A Supreme Court court ruling last month rejected the state’s previous congressional map as an illegal racial gerrymander and set off a redistricting race across the South.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Gerald Herbert/Associated Press, via Associated Press Photo, via Gerald Herbert
item.media:description: State Representative Edmond Jordan of Louisiana speaks with fellow lawmakers before a Louisiana House vote on a redistricting plan to eliminate a majority-Black congressional district in response to a U.S. Supreme Court ruling, in Baton Rouge, La., on Thursday.

item.title: Trump’s Counterterror Strategy Focuses on the Left
item.description: President Trump’s new counterterrorism strategy focuses on “violent left-wing extremists,” as well as narcoterrorists and Islamic terror groups. Our White House correspondent Zolan Kanno-Youngs explains what it means.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Gilad Thaler, Jon Miller, Stephanie Swart, Rafaela Balster, Whitney Shefte and Nikolay Nikolov

item.title: What Exactly Caused That Loud Boom in South Carolina?
item.description: A huge noise rattled part of the state on Thursday. Multiple theories have been put forward, but the mystery remains.
item.dc:creator: Victor Mather

item.title: Research Funding Slows Again for Universities Targeted by White House
item.description: The National Science Foundation lifted a hold on some grants for Harvard and other universities this week after inquiries from media outlets, including The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella, Mark Arsenault, Stephanie Saul and Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Harvard has faced more government actions than any school since President Trump returned to office.

item.title: After China Orders a Times Reporter to Leave the Country, the U.S. Reciprocates
item.description: The Trump administration ejected a Chinese journalist amid fresh concerns about Beijing’s growing pressure on foreign correspondents.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: U.S. and Chinese flags flying over Tiananmen Square during President Trump’s visit to Beijing this month.

item.title: Bus Crash in Virginia Kills 5 and Injures More Than 40
item.description: The bus was traveling in a southbound lane on I-95 near Quantico, Va., when it failed to slow for traffic in a work zone, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Virginia State Police, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A bus struck six vehicles on I-95 in Virginia early Friday morning, killing five people. More than three dozen others were injured, the authorities said.

item.title: ICE Agent Wanted in Minneapolis Shooting Is Captured in Texas
item.description: Christian Castro, who had been charged with assault in the shooting of a Venezuelan man during the immigration crackdown in Minnesota, was taken into custody Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Ernesto Londoño
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
item.media:description: Federal agents near the scene of the shooting in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: How Bad Are President Trump’s Approval Ratings?
item.description: Our chief political analyst, Nate Cohn, looks at President Trump’s sinking approval ratings. How do they compare with polling from other recent presidencies?
item.dc:creator: Nate Cohn, Laura Bult, June Kim, Edward Vega, Jon Miller and Whitney Shefte

item.title: Federal Judge Bars Trump From Immediately Setting Up $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: A judge in Virginia temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring money in or out of the fund until the court could hear arguments in June.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Erin Schaff/The New York Times
item.media:description: The order came from the Federal District Court for the Eastern District of Virginia, in Alexandria, in a case brought by a group of people who say they have faced partisan attacks by the Trump administration but expect to be excluded from accessing the fund.

item.title: Providence Isn’t an Official World Cup Host but It Sure Is Acting Like One
item.description: A stadium outside Boston that will host seven World Cup games is actually closer to Providence, R.I., prompting officials there to capitalize on that proximity to lure fans.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Kieran Dodds for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fans of the Scottish soccer team in Glasgow in 2021. Fans of Scotland’s World Cup team are expected to fill accommodations in Providence, R.I., to watch matches at a Massachusetts stadium next month.

item.title: Trans Athlete Could Repeat as State Champion at California Meet
item.description: Special rules will again be in place. Podium spots might be shared.
item.dc:creator: Juliet Macur
item.media:credit: Adam Perez for The New York Times
item.media:description: AB Hernandez, a senior at Jurupa Valley High School, is considered a strong contender to win three events at the state championships on Friday and Saturday in Clovis, Calif.

item.title: California House Primary in Sacramento Displays Democrats’ Fierce Generational Battle
item.description: Representative Doris Matsui has been re-elected 10 times with ease. But an upstart Democrat is threatening to end her run in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Doris Matsui is facing her toughest re-election fight in her more than two decades of representing a safely Democratic district in the Sacramento region.

item.title: Why Hasn’t California Elected a Woman Governor?
item.description: Most states have had a woman serve as governor, but California is in the minority despite its progressive-minded electorate. Women who have reached the top elsewhere saw familiar roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight, Soumya Karlamangla and Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Porter, a Democrat who served three terms in Congress, is the final prominent woman in the California governor’s race.
